llm.title: [GitHub] ollama/ollama: v0.18.3
llm.summary:
What's Changed

docs: update claude code and openclaw for web search and non-interactive mode by @ParthSareen in #14922

llm.title: [GitHub] ollama/ollama: v0.18.3-rc0: mlx: add mxfp4/mxfp8/nvfp4 importing (#15015)
llm.summary:
This change allows importing bf16 and converting to mxfp4/mxfp8/nvfp4
and also importing fp8 and converting directly to mxfp8.

llm.title: [GitHub] ollama/ollama: v0.18.2-rc1: launch: fix openclaw not picking up newly selected model (#14943)
llm.summary:
Sessions with a stale model field were not updated when the primary
changed, so the old model continued to be used.

llm.title: LiteLLM Hack: Were You One of the 47,000?
llm.summary:
LiteLLM Hack: Were You One of the 47,000?
Daniel Hnyk used the BigQuery PyPI dataset to determine how many downloads there were of the exploited LiteLLM packages during the 46 minute period they were live on PyPI.
They also identified 2,337 packages that depended on LiteLLM - 88% of which did not pin versions in a way that would have avoided the exploited version.

    Via @hnykda


    Tags: packaging, pypi, python, security, supply-chain

llm.title: Auto mode for Claude Code
llm.summary:
Auto mode for Claude Code
Really interesting new development in Claude Code today as an alternative to --dangerously-skip-permissions:

Today, we're introducing auto mode, a new permissions mode in Claude Code where Claude makes permission decisions on your behalf, with safeguards monitoring actions before they run.

Those safeguards appear to be implemented using Claude Sonnet 4.6, as described in the documentation:

Before each action runs, a separate classifier model reviews the conversation

llm.title: Package Managers Need to Cool Down
llm.summary:
Package Managers Need to Cool Down
Today's LiteLLM supply chain attack inspired me to revisit the idea of dependency cooldowns, the practice of only installing updated dependencies once they've been out in the wild for a few days to give the community a chance to spot if they've been subverted in some way.
This recent piece (March 4th) piece by Andrew Nesbitt reviews the current state of dependency cooldown mechanisms across different packaging tools. It's surprisingly well supported! There's be

I really think "give AI total control of my computer and therefore my entire life" is going to look so foolish in retrospect that everyone who went for this is going to look as dumb as Jimmy Fallon holding up a picture of his Bored Ape

top_articles.title: War and AI, the death of Sora, and 3 ways you can catch me live today
top_articles.summary: With apologies for the short notice, a few quick announcements:

top_articles.title: UK’s Starmer backs pause on crypto political donations, citing ‘illicit finance’ risks
top_articles.summary: The proposal would force UK political parties to unwind any crypto donations received once the rules take effect.

top_articles.title: Franklin Templeton taps Ondo Finance to tokenize five equities and gold ETFs
top_articles.summary: The new onchain products are geared for a crypto-native audience who prefer to access their investments through a digital wallet.

top_articles.title: The Daily: Circle selloff questioned as analysts defend outlook, Bhutan offloads $37M in BTC, Bitmine adds $145M in ETH, and more
top_articles.summary: The following article is adapted from The Block’s newsletter, The Daily, which comes out on weekday afternoons.

top_articles.title: Binance outlines market maker, token launch ‘red flags’ in updated trading rules
top_articles.summary: The update zeroes in on how market maker setups are structured, signaling that deal terms and trading behavior are now part of Binance’s scrutiny.

top_articles.title: ‘Big boy’ Morgan Stanley’s Bitcoin ETF launch likely ‘imminent’ says analyst
top_articles.summary: Eric Balchunas noted that the NYSE officially announced the listing of Morgan Stanley's spot Bitcoin ETF, a sign its launch is likely "imminent."

top_articles.title: Startale Group closes $63 million Series A with investments from SBI Group and Sony Innovation Fund
top_articles.summary: The company said it will use the capital to scale Layer 1 network Strium to power institutional tokenized securities and RWA trading.

top_articles.title: Stablecoin startup Payy, focused on private transactions, raises $6 million in seed funding
top_articles.summary: Payy's round was led by FirstMark Capital, an early backer of Airbnb, Shopify and Pinterest, and included Robot Ventures and DBA Crypto.

top_articles.title: Visa takes on Canton Super Validator role, marking major step in engaging with blockchain governance
top_articles.summary: The firm’s application was approved on March 23, three days after it was submitted, appointing Visa the highest Super Validator weight.

top_articles.title: Bitwise sees Circle worth $75 billion by 2030 as analysts defend stock after selloff
top_articles.summary: Circle shares fell about 20% on Tuesday amid reports that draft provisions in the CLARITY Act could limit rewards.

top_articles.title: Cipher Digital adds third AI lease, lines up $200 million credit facility as CIFR shares rise 10%
top_articles.summary: Cipher is increasingly leaning on debt-backed, flexible financing rather than equity issuance as it scales out its AI infrastructure.

top_articles.title: Tom Lee’s Bitmine debuts MAVAN Ethereum staking platform, plans to shift its ETH activity
top_articles.summary: Bitmine officially launched the MAVAN Ethereum staking platform, targeting roughly $300 million in annual rewards.

top_articles.title: Coinbase pushes order book, perps and futures data onchain with Chainlink’s DataLink bridge
top_articles.summary: Onchain protocols and apps will now be able to stream Coinbase's order book and derivatives trading data directly.

top_articles.title: Bitcoin consolidation points to potential market bottom as selling pressure wanes, K33 says
top_articles.summary: Head of Research Vetle Lunde said subdued derivatives activity and limited inflows point to a cautious market, but one forming a bottom.

top_articles.title: ‘Our interpretation is not an endpoint’: The SEC just clarified its crypto rules, but will they stick?
top_articles.summary: The SEC and CFTC have issued crypto interpretive guidance, but the big question now is whether that clarity has staying power.

top_articles.title: Beyond dollarization? Visa and Dune report finds non-USD stablecoins increasingly being used as money
top_articles.summary: The supply of non-USD stablecoins hit $1.1 billion in February, while aggregated transfer volume surged over 1,600%.

top_articles.title: Bitget Wallet debuts stablecoin payments infrastructure integrating with Ripple, Mastercard, Tether, and others
top_articles.summary: Bitget Wallet launched the Onchain Payments Matrix, connecting Ripple, Mastercard, and Tether within a global stablecoin payments network.

top_articles.title: Circle selloff may miss the mark as Clarity Act targets distributors, not issuers: Bernstein
top_articles.summary: Circle’s sharp stock drop may be overdone, Bernstein says, arguing investors are misinterpreting stablecoin yield restrictions.

top_articles.title: Kraken first to roll out access to STS Digital’s new crypto option structured products
top_articles.summary: Kraken is set to be the first platform to integrate STS’s structured products, according to an announcement on Wednesday.
